channel.title: Posts on Villa de Tara
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Villa de Tara

item.title: New Year 2024
item.description: The New Year has arrived, bringing with it a celebration of our first six months living in Crete. After spending the first part of last year planning and executing our move here, the second part was no less hectic! A lot has happened around the house since the summer, and I will update you with these over the next few days. There are more exciting changes to come which we will share with you as we move through the year.

item.title: Off to the South
item.description: Having visited Dive2gether for a look around back in April, we booked a couple of boat dives last week when we were in Plakias for a beach-day with visiting relatives. As we were going to be using our own equipment, they advised us they would want to test our BCDs and regulators. We arrived yesterday morning at 08:30 and met our guides for the day, Linda (who is part of the Marine Biology Research team) and Electra (who is currently on their Bootcamp programme).

item.title: An (Almost) Family Affair
item.description: Deborah’s daughter is staying with us for her summer holiday, accompanied by our grandson and her partner, Chris. He is a PADI-certified diver, but it has been several years since he last dived. When we mentioned the opportunity of diving during their visit, Chris was very excited and booked his PADI ReActivate immediately. We HAD to book our refresher dives! Last Wednesday, he and I went to Omega Divers to see what dives they had planned; they informed us they had a beach dive for the following day or a Lighthouse/Elephant’s Cave boat trip for yesterday.

item.title: A Refreshing Time
item.description: Deborah and I decided it had been far too long since we had been diving; our last ones were in October 2021! Eager to do things correctly, we booked our refresher dives with Omega Divers for this morning. After the mandatory form filling and waiting for the briefing for the Discover Scuba Diving participants to finish, we loaded our kit onto the pickup and boarded the minibus. We arrived at Koutalas Beach after about 10 minutes, driving down a very steep, narrow and winding track.

item.title: A Tale of Two Deliveries
item.description: We ordered some items at the IKEA order and delivery facility in Chania a couple of weeks ago. We chose delivery (in two to three weeks) because the main piece (a day bed for the study/third bedroom) wouldn’t fit in the car. I also ordered bathroom items from the Amazon German marketplace last Sunday with a delivery date of Tuesday 25 July. On Monday, I received a call from Delatolas Express Cargo telling me my IKEA delivery would be on Thursday.

item.title: Our Furniture Catches Up With Us
item.description: On Wednesday, I had a call from Spyros at NOMAD to say our furniture and house contents would be in Crete on Friday. We agreed that they could deliver on the same day. However, he sent another message on Thursday to say the furniture was coming by the day boat, arriving at 20:30. We agreed that Saturday morning would be OK, early, to try and avoid the extreme heat due to the Cerberus heatwave.

item.title: First Use of the Greek Health System
item.description: Deborah got an insect bite on her chest at the end of last week. Over the past couple of days it has become more inflamed and a rash has spread out from it. This morning she decided that she wanted to see a doctor. Armed with her European Health Insurance Card (EHIC) we set off for Vamos Health Centre. When we arrived we saw that there was sign asking all visitors to wear a face make.

item.title: Moving Days
item.description: What a week it has been! After what seems like months of packing all our possessions into boxes, NOMAD turned up on Tuesday to take it all to Peterborough. From there they will transport everything to Patra in Greece and then onward to Villa de Tara via their depot in Chania. The guys did a fantastic job packing the bits we hadn’t (beds, lamps, pictures and mirrors) and loading them into their truck.

item.title: Turning Up the Heat
item.description: Crete is very hot in the summer but it can be very cold in the winter - they don’t call them the White Mountains for nothing! Although the pipework is in place for a boiler and radiators, there is no heating in the house. As we are planning to live here all year round something needed to be done. Which is why I was in Crete, in August, watching the guys from YdroTherm in Vamos knock out the old, inefficient fireplace and install a brand-new wood-burner.

item.title: Collecting Our Residency Cards
item.description: Kostas, our property lawyer, had informed us back in December that our residency cards were ready. However, this was our first chance to collect them. This time we did not require an appointment and was a much quicker process than the initial one; we just had to show our passports and the temporary cards were swapped for the new, permanent ones.

item.title: COSMOTE Steps Up
item.description: When we arrived at the house on Friday, we noticed that the broadband router only had the power light on. There was Wi-Fi but no Internet service. When I power-cycled it, the other lights illuminated as they should but then went out. We had planned to sort out Greek mobile numbers for ourselves and went into Chania on Saturday morning, looking for COSMOTE shops as we passed through. Having parked in probably the tightest underground car park I’ve ever been in, we returned to the closest.

item.title: Having Our Fingerprints Taken
item.description: The Greek Golden Visa Program grants a five-year residence permit in exchange for a minimum €250,000 property investment in Greece (this is in Crete; other areas have higher requirements). We had informed our property lawyer, Kostas, of our intention to apply for these when we first spoke about the purchase. He informed us that the purchase transaction would have to go through our Greek bank account, even though the sellers were based in the UK too.

item.title: It All Starts Here
item.description: We first saw Villa de Tara in October 2020 after our first attempt at house-hunting in Crete was halted by the COVID-19 Delta variant. Although it wasn’t our first choice, we were unable to agree a deal for our preferred property and quickly moved for this one. On reflection, Villa de Tara offers us a much better option in terms of house layout, location and access to local services. The property finally became ours back in May this year and we are now looking forward to planning our future here.
